comments.body: Implemented RED baseline in tests/unit/test_llm_failure_matrix.py (31 tests, all green on today's code). Committed a63ae39cc atop the stack (rebar-ticket trailer, Change-Id present). Pins: runner-level classification matrix (18 cases: HTTP/timeout/refusal/incomplete->LLMUnavailableError, canned content_filter+length finish_reason->UnretryableOutputError, unparseable->StructuredOutputError, model-raised UsageLimitExceeded->LLMRunnerError); derived UsageLimits(request_limit=max(1,ceil(mi/2)),tool_calls_limit=max(8,mi)) via spy; gate two-scope (systemic->INDETERMINATE unsigned; ladder re-raises systemic, drops non-systemic finder fail-open); guards (text mode ignores finish_reason; disabled code-review never invokes runner); meta-test enforces CURRENT(<file>:<line>) marker per row. lint+format+typecheck clean. Not closing until Verified +1 at endgame.

compiled_state.description:
RED baseline test suite: inject the full LLM-failure matrix through the pydantic-ai `model_override` seam and pin rebar's CURRENT behavior, so later stories flip it to the new behavior with a regression net.

## Why
Before changing behavior we must pin what rebar does today for every failure class and prove the offline injection harness works. Injection is at the pydantic-ai MODEL layer (a `FunctionModel` that raises or returns canned responses); a raised exception (e.g. `ModelHTTPError(429)`, `httpx.ReadTimeout`) propagates UNWRAPPED through the pydantic-ai model/agent layer up to the runner's `except` seam (`runner.py:353-365`), where the runner TODAY wraps it into `LLMUnavailableError` â€” so the runner-level CURRENT behavior this suite pins is that wrap, and the model-layer un-wrapped propagation is what makes the offline injection observable. A canned `finish_reason` is readable at `result.response.finish_reason`.

## Verified library behavior (experiment log â€” pydantic-ai 1.107.0, offline)
Run `python <script>` â€” captured ACTUAL output (proves the matrix's library assumptions; disproves any "symbol absent" reading):
```
pydantic-ai-slim version: 1.107.0
[1] pydantic_ai.exceptions symbols resolve:
    pydantic_ai.exceptions.ModelHTTPError  <: ['ModelAPIError', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    pydantic_ai.exceptions.ContentFilterError  <: ['UnexpectedModelBehavior', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    pydantic_ai.exceptions.IncompleteToolCall  <: ['UnexpectedModelBehavior', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    pydantic_ai.exceptions.UsageLimitExceeded  <: ['AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError', 'Exception']
    ContentFilterError is UnexpectedModelBehavior subclass: True
    IncompleteToolCall is UnexpectedModelBehavior subclass: True
[2] injected ModelHTTPError propagated: status_code=429 type=ModelHTTPError
[3] canned finish_reason readable: result.response.finish_reason='length'
[4] httpx.ReadTimeout propagated unwrapped: ReadTimeout: read timed out
[5] max_iterations=6 -> request_limit=3, tool_calls_limit=8; =250 -> request_limit=125, tool_calls_limit=250
[6] runner collapses injected 429 -> LLMUnavailableError (current behavior)
```
So `ContentFilterError` and `IncompleteToolCall` ARE valid, importable `pydantic_ai.exceptions` classes (subclasses of `UnexpectedModelBehavior`) â€” they are pydantic-ai LIBRARY symbols, not rebar symbols, so they will NOT appear in a grep of the rebar repo; a code-grounding pass restricted to the rebar snapshot must not read their repo-absence as non-existence (tracked as bug succinct-formable-kite). `[4]`+`[6]` together show the two-layer reality: unwrapped at the model layer, wrapped at the runner seam.

## What
A parametrized RED test file injecting each failure class via a raising or canned pydantic-ai `FunctionModel` through the `PydanticAIRunner(model_override=...)` / `get_runner(override=...)` seams (`src/rebar/llm/runner.py`), asserting current behavior at two levels: (1) runner classification and (2) gate end-to-end. THE TEST IS THE SOURCE OF TRUTH for current behavior â€” it pins whatever typed error the runner ACTUALLY raises for each class (discovered empirically by running the injection and asserting the observed exception type/verdict exactly), rather than this description pre-asserting a specific type. At the gate level it pins the observed current outcome per gate (e.g. a systemic failure yields plan-review's degraded/INDETERMINATE verdict + a blocked claim, completion's fail-closed, and code-review's degraded outcome â€” each asserted as-observed). Guarded by `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`.

## Scope
- Failure matrix: 429-rate, 429-insufficient_quota (body), 529, 500/503, connect-timeout, read-timeout, 401/403, 400 bad-request, 400 context-length, 413, `content_filter` finish_reason, a structured content-filter refusal, length/truncation, an incomplete-tool-call, unparseable output, validation-retry exhaustion, step-budget overrun, tool-call-limit overrun, unknown exception. The two content-filter cases use DIFFERENT injection mechanisms and are NOT the same test:
  - **`content_filter` finish_reason case** â€” the model returns a CANNED `ModelResponse(finish_reason="content_filter")`. This RAISES NO EXCEPTION; it exercises `structured.check_stop_reason` reading the finish_reason. (No `ContentFilterError` involved.)
  - **structured content-filter refusal case** â€” the model RAISES `pydantic_ai.exceptions.ContentFilterError` (an `UnexpectedModelBehavior` subclass, verified above). This is an exception-propagation path, entirely separate from the canned-finish_reason case above.
  The incomplete-tool-call case likewise RAISES `pydantic_ai.exceptions.IncompleteToolCall`. The test imports these types at runtime â€” an absent symbol fails collection loudly (that IS the version-drift signal).
- Two-scope scenarios: systemic (whole op) vs per-criterion (one unit among N; the plan-review pass-1 fail-open, where one flaky finder marks its criterion INDETERMINATE and the review continues).
- Named liveness scenarios pin CURRENT (pre-change) behavior only. TODAY the only model-call bound is the COARSE per-call wall-clock `timeout` (`cfg.timeout_s` â†’ `model_settings["timeout"]`, `runner.py:285`, default 600s, the Anthropic/httpx request timeout); there is NO per-TOOL execution timeout and NO activity-based read timeout. The hung-model scenario pins that a stalled request is bounded ONLY by that coarse call timeout, and the hung-tool scenario pins that a stalled tool is UNbounded today (asserted via a short outer test-level guard, since the RED test cannot wait 600s); a looping model reaches the runner's DERIVED request budget. Story chief-contained-hoopoe's tests replace these with the activity-based read/tool-timeout + step-cap assertions â€” NOT here; this baseline must stay green on today's code.
- The step-budget/tool-call-limit cases set `cfg.max_iterations` explicitly and assert against the DERIVED limits the runner computes â€” `request_limit = max(1, ceil(cfg.max_iterations/2))` and `tool_calls_limit = max(8, cfg.max_iterations)` (runner.py, verified in `[5]`) â€” NOT pydantic-ai's default 50.
- Guard tests (each a distinct mechanism, not one concept): `--force` (the claim bypass flag) and `--force-close` (the close bypass flag) each cause their gate to SKIP the gate LLM call entirely; and the feature-disabled config path (the completion-verification / signature close-gate config toggled off) likewise skips it. TODAY there is no dedicated failure-handling code to "enter", so each guard pins that the injected `FunctionModel` is NEVER invoked on these paths (asserted by a model that raises if called) â€” the invariant later stories preserve when the handling code is added.
- Coverage boundary: `model_override` injects at the pydantic-ai MODEL layer, which is ABOVE the httpx transport where the retry lives â€” so transport-layer retry is NOT exercisable here; it is covered by the transport-injection spike in story morbid-uncultured-arcticduck. This suite covers classification + gate behavior + silent-success, not the transport.
- NOT in scope: the NEW behavior (asserted GREEN by later stories) â€” this story pins CURRENT behavior only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A parametrized test file (e.g. `tests/unit/test_llm_failure_matrix.py`) exercises every failure class via `model_override`, offline, guarded by `ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS=False`.
- [ ] Each case pins the current runner-level and gate-level behavior with an inline `# CURRENT(<file>:<line>): <one-line behavior>` comment citing the exact source seam it pins; a meta-test asserts a `# CURRENT(` marker exists for each parametrized id (that inline comment IS the "source citation" â€” documentation of the pinned seam, verified by the meta-test, not a runtime assertion).
- [ ] The step-budget / tool-call-limit cases set `cfg.max_iterations` explicitly and assert against the runner's DERIVED `request_limit`/`tool_calls_limit`, never a hardcoded 50.
- [ ] Two-scope (systemic vs per-criterion) and force/disabled guard scenarios are covered.
- [ ] `make test` passes; the file is the named baseline that later stories flip to the new behavior.
compiled_state.title: RED baseline: inject the LLM-failure matrix via model_override and pin current behavior
